req.title: Reset employee password — variant 2
req.statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-103 specifies five testable behaviours: (1) successful password reset with a valid token, (2) rejection of an expired token (>30 min), (3) rejection of a password that fails policy, (4) session invalidation after reset, and (5) idempotency under retry. I mapped these to five test cases covering EP (happy-path partition), NEG (expired token, policy violation), BVA (token at exactly the 30-minute boundary), and STC (session state transition + idempotency retry). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down tokens and sessions.

tests.name: test_reset_password_with_valid_token_succeeds
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists for employee@example.com
The new password satisfies the password policy
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token and policy-compliant new password
Attempt login with the new password
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reset_password_with_valid_token_succeeds(
    api_client,          # <fixture> pre-configured requests.Session for hr-app
    fresh_reset_token,   # <fixture> yields (token, employee_email, old_password)
):
    token, email, old_pw = fresh_reset_token
    new_pw = "NewP@ssw0rd!99"

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": token, "password": new_pw},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "updated"

    login_new = api_client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"email": email, "password": new_pw},
    )
    assert login_new.status_code == 200

    login_old = api_client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"email": email, "password": old_pw},
    )
    assert login_old.status_code == 401

tests.name: test_reset_password_with_expired_token_returns_410
preconditions: A reset token that was issued more than 30 minutes ago exists
The token has not been used
actions: POST /password/reset with the expired token and a policy-compliant password
expected: Response body contains an error indicating the link has expired
trace: R-WEB-103: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reset_password_with_expired_token_returns_410(
    api_client,           # <fixture> pre-configured requests.Session for hr-app
    expired_reset_token,  # <fixture> yields a token whose TTL has elapsed (>30 min)
):
    token = expired_reset_token
    new_pw = "NewP@ssw0rd!99"

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": token, "password": new_pw},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 410
    body = resp.json()
    assert "expired" in body.get("error", "").lower()

tests.name: test_reset_password_at_30_minute_boundary_is_rejected
preconditions: A reset token whose age is exactly 30 minutes (1800 seconds) exists
actions: POST /password/reset with the boundary-age token and a policy-compliant password
expected: HTTP 410 returned (token expired at exactly T=30 min)
trace: R-WEB-103: link expires after 30 minutes — boundary at 1800 s is expired
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reset_password_at_30_minute_boundary_is_rejected(
    api_client,                 # <fixture> pre-configured requests.Session for hr-app
    token_aged_exactly_30_min,  # <fixture> yields (token, email, old_password)
):
    token, email, old_pw = token_aged_exactly_30_min
    new_pw = "BoundaryP@ss1!"

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": token, "password": new_pw},
    )
    # At T==1800s the token is expired per the requirement
    assert resp.status_code == 410

    # Confirm password was not changed
    login_old = api_client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"email": email, "password": old_pw},
    )
    assert login_old.status_code == 200

tests.name: test_reset_password_failing_policy_returns_422
preconditions: A valid, unexpired reset token exists
The candidate password does NOT satisfy the password policy (e.g. too short)
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token and policy-violating password
expected: Response body contains a policy-violation error message
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_password", [
    "short",       # too short
    "alllowercase1!",  # no uppercase
    "ALLUPPERCASE1!",  # no lowercase
    "NoSpecialChar1",  # no special character
])
def test_reset_password_failing_policy_returns_422(
    api_client,         # <fixture> pre-configured requests.Session for hr-app
    fresh_reset_token,  # <fixture> yields (token, email, old_password)
    bad_password,
):
    token, email, old_pw = fresh_reset_token

    resp = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": token, "password": bad_password},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "password" in body.get("error", "").lower()

    login_old = api_client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"email": email, "password": old_pw},
    )
    assert login_old.status_code == 200

tests.name: test_reset_password_invalidates_sessions_and_is_idempotent
preconditions: Employee has at least one active session (session_token_before)
A valid, unexpired reset token exists for that employee
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token and new password (first attempt)
POST /password/reset again with the same token and same new password (retry)
expected: Old session token is rejected with HTTP 401 after reset
Second POST (retry) returns HTTP 200 — idempotent, no error raised
Password remains the new value after retry
trace: R-WEB-103: action must be idempotent under retry
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_reset_password_invalidates_sessions_and_is_idempotent(
    api_client,           # <fixture> pre-configured requests.Session for hr-app
    employee_with_session, # <fixture> yields (token, email, old_pw, session_bearer)
):
    token, email, old_pw, session_bearer = employee_with_session
    new_pw = "IdempotentP@ss2!"
    headers_old = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {session_bearer}"}

    first = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": token, "password": new_pw},
    )
    assert first.status_code == 200

    session_check = api_client.get("/me", headers=headers_old)
    assert session_check.status_code == 401

    second = api_client.post(
        "/password/reset",
        json={"token": token, "password": new_pw},
    )
    assert second.status_code == 200

    login_new = api_client.post(
        "/auth/login",
        json={"email": email, "password": new_pw},
    )
    assert login_new.status_code == 200
